QF.id: XIII Trading, Clearing, Settlement and Risk Management_35

question.text: How does the settlement mechanism differ between currency derivatives and equity stock futures in India?

answer.text: Currency derivatives are exclusively cash-settled in INR, while equity stock futures are physically settled through delivery of underlying shares
Both currency and equity stock futures are cash-settled — physical delivery applies only to commodity derivatives
Currency derivatives are settled in the respective foreign currency, while equity stock futures are cash-settled in INR
Both segments offer the choice of physical or cash settlement at the option of the buyer

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Currency derivatives in India are mandatorily cash-settled in INR — no foreign currency changes hands. Equity stock futures and options have moved to compulsory physical settlement, where actual shares are delivered. Index derivatives remain cash-settled. **The Trap:** Currency derivatives are settled in INR, NOT in the foreign currency. Also, equity stock F&O is now physically settled (not cash-settled). Index derivatives (Nifty, Bank Nifty) remain cash-settled. **Cross-Exam Context:** Settlement modes across segments: Currency (all cash/INR), Equity index (cash), Equity stock (physical delivery), Interest rate bond futures (physical delivery via conversion factor or cash via FSP). **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Physical settlement of stock derivatives was introduced to curb excessive speculation and improve price convergence. Traders must ensure adequate shares/funds for delivery obligations near expiry.
